theme: clock a mystery CPU with a stopwatch
title: Clock the Mystery Pipeline — recovering a compounding structural-hazard law
strategies: trivial: emit the constant mean(train cycles) -> reproduces the checker's own baseline, ~0.1

greedy: additive per-opcode/per-hazard-count linear regression cycles ~ b0 + b1*n + b2*cLU + b3*cMF + b4*cST, fit by least squares on the training table; near-perfect on train (the training probes only ever contain 0-2 adjacent same-unit MUL pairs, where the true law is nearly linear) but the shared multiply unit's single-server backlog is a TRIANGULAR (quadratic) function of the run length, not a per-pair constant, so this model catastrophically underestimates the long hazard-dense held-out runs

strong: recognize that a non-pipelined shared functional unit hit by a run of R back-to-back consumers backlogs like a single-server queue (service time > arrival interval), so the correct regressor is the triangular number cST*(cST+1)/2, not cST itself; refit the SAME linear regression with that one feature swapped in -> the fitted coefficients transfer correctly to the long, hazard-dense held-out probes

headroom above strong: jointly re-derive the exact integer coefficients (2, 1, 1) from first principles instead of a noisy least-squares fit, and model the measurement jitter's distribution to shave the residual held-out error further
